2604.11365.title: Learning from Contrasts: Synthesizing Reasoning Paths from Diverse Search Trajectories
2604.11365.paper_summary: Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) has been widely used for automated reasoning data exploration, but current supervision extraction methods remain inefficient. Standard approaches retain only the single highest-reward trajectory, discarding the comparative signals present in the many explored paths. Here we introduce \textbf{Contrastive Reasoning Path Synthesis (CRPS)}, a framework that transforms supervision extraction from a filtering process into a synthesis procedure. CRPS uses a structured reflective process to analyze the differences between high- and low-quality search trajectories, extracting explicit information about strategic pivots and local failure modes. These insights guide the synthesis of reasoning chains that incorporate success patterns while avoiding identified pitfalls. We show empirically that models fine-tuned on just 60K CRPS-synthesized examples match or exceed the performance of baselines trained on 590K examples derived from standard rejection sampling, a 20$\times$ reduction in dataset size. Furthermore, CRPS improves generalization on out-of-domain benchmarks, demonstrating that learning from the contrast between success and failure produces more transferable reasoning capabilities than learning from success alone.
2604.11365.paper_summary_zh: 蒙地卡羅樹搜尋（MCTS）已被廣泛應用於自動推理數據探索，但目前的監督提取方法仍然效率低下。標準方法僅保留單一的最高獎勵軌跡，忽略了許多探索路徑中存在的比較信號。在此，我們介紹\textbf{對比推理路徑合成（CRPS）}，這是一個將監督提取從過濾過程轉變為合成程序的框架。CRPS使用結構化的反思過程來分析高質量和低質量搜尋軌跡之間的差異，提取有關戰略轉折點和局部失敗模式的明確信息。這些見解指導推理鏈的合成，這些推理鏈結合了成功模式，同時避免已識別的陷阱。我們實證顯示，僅在60K CRPS合成範例上進行微調的模型，其性能與在590K來自標準拒絕抽樣的範例上訓練的基準相匹配或超越，數據集大小減少了20$\times$。此外，CRPS在域外基準上改善了泛化能力，顯示從成功與失敗之間的對比中學習，產生的可轉移推理能力超過僅從成功中學習的能力。
2604.11365.author: Peiyang Liu et.al.
2604.11365.authors: Peiyang Liu, Zhirui Chen, Xi Wang, Di Liang, Youru Li, Zhi Cai, Wei Ye
